beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and tend rooftop herb garden in morning light.; Quick breakfast and pack lunch to save money.
beats.reasoning: Monday morning with full energy; sunny weather is perfect for checking on herbs before the workday. Rooftop garden fits Juniper's urban gardening passion and organized, weather-aware personality.

beats.activity: Commute by bus to Capitol Hill workplace, avoiding Light Rail closure (Westlake–U District down 9am–3pm).
beats.reasoning: Light Rail closure forces bus route; Green Lake to Capitol Hill is ~6 km, reasonable 35-min bus commute. Scheduled before 9am to avoid closure impact.

beats.activity: Check equipment, review wind site data, and prepare for field work.
beats.reasoning: Wind turbine technician office base in Capitol Hill; organized routine of equipment checks before field deployment. Ends at 11am when FIFA match airs, but Juniper prioritizes work over crowds and doesn't attend bars.

beats.activity: Skip FIFA match; grab quick lunch at desk while reviewing site notes.
beats.reasoning: Juniper is frugal and prefers close friends over big crowds; downtown bars during FIFA don't fit personality. Uses lunch break productively to prep for afternoon field work.

beats.activity: Commute to wind turbine site in Snoqualmie Pass region.
beats.reasoning: Capitol Hill to Snoqualmie Pass is ~60 km; across-town commute warrants 60 min. Afternoon field work aligns with wind technician job profile. Sunny weather is ideal for high-altitude work.

beats.activity: Perform turbine maintenance and blade inspection at site.
beats.reasoning: Core job function; 4.5-hour field shift is realistic for technician work. Sunny 74F weather is favorable for outdoor high-altitude tasks. Returns before evening.

beats.activity: Commute back to Seattle; stop at QFC for fresh produce and herbs.
beats.reasoning: Snoqualmie to Green Lake is ~50 km; 75 min covers drive and quick stop. Shopping at neighborhood QFC fits frugal nature and urban gardening passion. Ends near home.

beats.activity: Prepare dinner with fresh ingredients; tend rooftop garden in evening light.
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down at home; cooking with fresh produce aligns with gardening hobby and organized lifestyle. Sunny evening is perfect for garden maintenance and relaxation.

beats.activity: Paint in home studio while listening to music; reflect on the day.
beats.reasoning: Juniper paints as a creative hobby; evening leisure time at home fits personality. Quiet solo activity aligns with preference for close friends over crowds and organized self-care.

beats.activity: Wind down, prepare for bed, and sleep.
beats.reasoning: Reasonable 8.5-hour sleep after full workday in field. Ends at 06:30 next morning, well before 06:00 cutoff for next day. Overnight beat visibly spans midnight on map replay.

diary: Woke to sunlight hitting the rooftop garden—basil and mint looking good this year. Grabbed toast and coffee, then caught the bus to Capitol Hill, grateful I'd checked the Light Rail schedule yesterday. The site data looked clean, so I headed out to Snoqualmie Pass around 11. The weather was perfect for blade work, and I spent most of the afternoon up there inspecting sections, hands getting that familiar ache. Stopped at QFC on the way home for fresh tomatoes and some new herb seedlings. Made a simple dinner with what I picked, then spent a couple hours in the studio with some music—nothing fancy, just letting the brush do what it wanted. The USA-Wales match was happening downtown, but honestly, I wasn't feeling the crowds today. Just needed the quiet of home.
thought_process: I made a deliberate choice to skip the match and the bar scene, even though it would've been easy to join people downtown. Part of it was practical—I needed to review site notes—but mostly I think I was protecting my headspace. The turbine work is satisfying but demanding, and after being up high all afternoon, I wanted to ground myself. The Light Rail closure didn't really affect me since I'd planned around it, which felt good. Tending the garden twice, cooking fresh, painting... it all felt intentional today, like I was choosing slowness even though the world was buzzing with World Cup energy.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
